title: As the United States Turns 250, Americans Must Stand Up for Democracy and Freedom
content:
Visitors in the Rotunda of the National Archives building in Washington, DC view the Charters of Freedom. (Photo Credit: U.S. National Archives)

On July 4, 1776, the United States’ founders declared before the world a new nation grounded in the belief that all men are endowed with unalienable rights that no unaccountable power may extinguish. Trusting in the judgement of free people, they launched one of history’s greatest experiments: a democratic system based on the will and consent of citizens. Novel at the time, it is a concept that transformed the world. 

Shortly before his inauguration in 1861, Abraham Lincoln said that the Declaration “gave liberty, not alone to the people of this country, but, I hope, to the world, for all future time. It was that which gave promise that in due time the weight would be lifted from the shoulders of all men."

As the United States marks its 250th anniversary, Lincoln’s prophesy has been proven true. The principles our Republic was founded upon remain as consequential—but as contested—as ever.
snippet: Visitors in the Rotunda of the National Archives building in Washington, DC view the Charters of Freedom. (Photo Credit: U.S. National Archives)
creator: zevans@freedomhouse.org

title: TNR Watch: European Parliament Tackles Transnational Repression
content:
Resolution adopted: On June 16, the European Parliament (EP) adopted a landmark resolution that calls on all member states to unite to combat the threat posed by transnational repression. The resolution, which is based on a report commissioned by European Parliament member Hannah Neumann, who sits on the EP’s Foreign Affairs Committee, recommends that the European Union establish a definition of transnational repression, create a data-collection and reporting mechanism for it, strengthen protection measures for and outreach to targeted individuals, and increase accountability for perpetrator governments.

This vote came seven months after the EP approved a separate resolution on transnational repression against human rights defenders and just weeks after the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) and the EP convened a meeting of the Human Rights Defenders Protection Community of Practice that, among other issues, examined the threat posed to human rights defenders by transnational repression.

creator: zevans@freedomhouse.org

title: Venezuela's Transition: From Stabilization to Democracy
content:
Join Freedom House for a conversation with Venezuelan opposition leader and co-founder of the World Liberty Congress Leopoldo López on the state of Venezuela's transition, the daily realities facing Venezuelans, and the role that the United States and its partners can play in supporting a peaceful, democratic, and sustainable future for the country and its people.

Background: As Venezuela begins to recover from the devastating earthquake that has compounded an already severe humanitarian and economic crisis, the country also finds itself navigating an unprecedented and uncertain political chapter following the removal of Nicolás Maduro. Attention is increasingly turning to what comes next for the Venezuelan people. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has outlined a three-phase framework—stabilization, recovery, and transition—focused on restoring order, rebuilding economic opportunity and democratic institutions, and ultimately creating the conditions for free and fair elections. As policymakers assess progress and remaining obstacles, key questions remain about how to move from stabilization to a genuine democratic transition. At the same time, ordinary Venezuelans continue to confront significant economic and social challenges, including strained public services, limited economic opportunity, and the lasting effects of years of authoritarian rule and economic decline. The earthquake has not only underscored the importance of resilient institutions capable of responding to immediate humanitarian needs, but also the importance of laying the foundation for long-term recovery.

creator: zevans@freedomhouse.org

title: Don’t Leave the Iranian People Behind
content:
President Donald Trump signs the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding between the Islamic Republic of Iran and the United States at the Palace of Versailles, France. (Photo Credit: The White House)

In early 2026, when Iranian surgeon Alireza Golchini saw protesters with gunshot and stab wounds from the regime, he did what doctors are trained to do: he treated them. For that, Iranian authorities arrested him, charged him with “waging war against God,” and threatened him with a sentence that can carry the death penalty. His alleged crime was not violence or espionage; it was providing medical care to wounded Iranians.

His story is a reminder of a simple reality: the Islamic Republic is one of the world’s most repressive regimes. Just recently, a woman was sentenced to 74 lashes for singing in a YouTube video without a hijab, and a 17-year-old swimmer and boxer was sentenced to death for participating in a protest. The regime just executed another dissident, bringing the total number of political executions in 2026 to at least 45.
snippet: President Donald Trump signs the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding between the Islamic Republic of Iran and the United States at the Palace of Versailles, France. (Photo Credit: The White House)
creator: zevans@freedomhouse.org

title: No Reset Without Releases: Georgia’s Political Prisoners and the Price of Better Relations with Washington
content:
Demonstrators march in April 2025 with posters supporting Georgian prisoners of conscience. (Photo Credit: Sebastien Canaud, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0)

In recent months, Georgian officials have signaled a desire to improve ties with the Trump administration. Members of the Georgian Dream government have pointed to renewed diplomatic contacts and commercially driven initiatives—including plans for a 70-story Trump Tower Tbilisi—as signs that relations with Washington may be improving after several years of tension.
snippet: Demonstrators march in April 2025 with posters supporting Georgian prisoners of conscience. (Photo Credit: Sebastien Canaud, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0)
creator: zevans@freedomhouse.org

title: The 2026 Mark Palmer Prize: Honoring H.E. Salome Zourabichvili
content:
On June 15, 2026, Freedom House hosted a special event honoring the fifth president of Georgia, H.E. Salome Zourabichvili, as the recipient of the 2026 Mark Palmer Prize, recognizing her principled leadership and steadfast commitment to democratic values during a pivotal moment for Georgia and the broader struggle for freedom in Europe. Named for the late Ambassador Mark Palmer—a longtime Freedom House trustee and leading advocate for democratic transitions around the world—the prize honors diplomats and public servants who have gone beyond the normal course of their duties to defend fundamental freedoms, support civil society, and strengthen democratic institutions. President Zourabichvili’s principled leadership and steadfast support for democratic governance, civic freedoms, and Georgia’s Euro-Atlantic future have made her a powerful voice for democracy at a time of mounting pressure on free societies worldwide. The evening brought together diplomats, policymakers, civil society leaders, and members of the international democracy community to celebrate the enduring importance of courageous, values-driven leadership in the global struggle for freedom.

title: Freedom House Welcomes New Board Members Margaret Hoover, David Pressman
content:
WASHINGTON—Freedom House is pleased to announce that journalist Margaret Hoover and former US Ambassador to Hungary David Pressman are joining the Freedom House Board.

“On behalf of the entire Freedom House Board, I am thrilled to welcome Margaret Hoover and Ambassador David Pressman as our newest board members. They are recognized leaders in their fields, who bring unique skills, perspectives, and networks in support of this storied organization. As Freedom House celebrates our 85th anniversary, the organization stands at an exciting moment to take major steps to realize our vision of a world where all are free. We look forward to working with Margaret and David to expand Freedom House’s impact in this critical moment,” said Freedom House Board Chair Norman Willox.
snippet: WASHINGTON—Freedom House is pleased to announce that journalist Margaret Hoover and former US Ambassador to Hungary David Pressman are joining the Freedom House Board.
creator: zevans@freedomhouse.org

title: TNR Watch: Canceling RightsCon is not Transnational Repression, But It Is an Attack on Transnational Civil Society
content: Under pressure: In late April, the digital rights organization Access Now announced that its annual RightsCon conference scheduled to take place in Lusaka, Zambia, from May 5 to May 8 had been called off due to pressure from the Chinese government.* The announcement came after Zambian officials had abruptly backtracked on their commitment to host the event—considered the premier civil society gathering on human rights and technology—because Chinese government officials reportedly objected to planned discussion of China’s digital authoritarian practices and the participation of Taiwanese activists. In a statement, Access Now said it was unwilling to “moderate specific topics and exclude communities at risk, including our Taiwanese participants, from in-person and online participation.”
